channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gematsu.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: Lords of the Fallen II delayed to Q1 2027
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Switch 2
  • Xbox Series

No longer due out this fall.

Publisher CI Games and developer Hexworks have delayed Soulslike action RPG Lords of the Fallen II from its previously planned fall 2026 release window to Q1 2027. It will be available for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, Switch 2, and PC via Steam and Epic Games Store.

Here is the full message from CI Games founder and CE Marek Tyminski on the delay:

Lords of the Fallen II is now set for a Q1 2027 release window.

As development progresses, we have worked closely with the Gameplay Feedback Team, a dedicated group of seasoned Souls-like veterans within our Launch Creative Team. Their valuable ongoing input, combined with the team’s vision, has identified meaningful opportunities to further refine and strengthen the overall experience.

These enhancements will benefit from additional integration, iteration, and polishing time, enabling the team to deliver the highest possible quality at launch.

This updated release window also strategically positions Lords of the Fallen II outside of a highly competitive holiday period, ensuring the game receives the dedicated attention it deserves.

The team remains fully energized by the strong response to our recent reveals and is deeply focused on delivering a standout dark fantasy action RPG and a worthy successor to the Lords of the Fallen franchise. We are grateful for the continued passion, feedback, and support from our community, and we look forward to sharing more updates in the coming months.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/lords-of-the-fallen-ii-delayed-to-q1-2027
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds free character updates Classic Sonic, Axel, and Amigo announced
item.content:encoded:
SEGA has announced new playable characters coming to Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds from today until September, including Classic Sonic from Sonic the Hedgehog, Axel from the Crazy Taxi series, and Amigo from the Samba de Amgio series.

The full list of upcoming free content is as follows:

  • Classic Sonic (June 23, 2026)
    • Classic Sonic from Sonic the Hedgehog as a playable character
    • Exclusive vehicle
  • Axel (August 2026)
    • Axel from the Crazy Taxi series as a playable character
    • Exclusive vehicle
  • Amigo (September 2026)
    • Amigo from the Samba de Amgio series as a playable character
    • Exclusive vehicle

Nine new songs are also coming to the in-game jukebox, including:

  • “Looting the Louvre” by Naofumi Hataya (from Rhythm Thief & the Emperor’s Treasure)
  • “Theme of Phantom R” by Tomoya Ohtani (from Rhythm Thief & the Emperor’s Treasure)
  • “Akadoko” by Naofumi Hataya (from Akadoko & Kimishine Ai no Densetsu CD)
  • “Orpheus in the Underworld” by Naofumi Hataya (from Akadoko & Kimishine Ai no Densetsu CD)
  • “Kimishine” by Naofumi Hataya (from Akadoko & Kimishine Ai no Densetsu CD)
  • “Speed Is My Life” by Jun Senoue and Nathan Sharp (from the Sonic the Hedgehog 35th anniversary)
  • “space channel 5: getting the truth” by Kenichi Tokoi (from Space Channel 5)
  • “Go! Go! Cheerleader!” by SEGA Sound Team (from Space Channel 5)
  • “Burning Hearts” by Naofumi Hataya and Takenobu Mitsuyoshi (from Burning Rangers)

Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds is available now for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, Switch 2, PlayStation 4, Xbox One, Switch, and PC via Steam and Epic Games Store.

Watch a new trailer below.

New Free Characters Update #3

English



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/sonic-racing-crossworlds-free-character-updates-classic-sonic-axel-and-amigo-announced
